title: 1960s America
description: Test your knowledge of the tumult, war, and upheaval of the 1960s.

assessment_items.question_content:
What were the major tenets of John F. Kennedy’s presidential platform? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Anti-communism was a key part of John F. Kennedy's platform.

choices.content: Anti-communism, civil rights, and public sector spending
Fiscal responsibility, states’ rights, and segregation
Anti-communism, anti-corruption, and anti-immigration

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Great Society** initiative was a series of programs and legislation that aimed to

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: eradicate poverty in the United States.
eradicate alcohol consumption in the United States.
promote literacy in the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
What were the implications of Cuba possessing **nuclear armaments**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Following World War II, the United States and the Soviet Union were the only two superpowers in the world.

choices.content: Missiles from Cuba could hit major targets in the United States: not just Miami, but New York City and Washington, D.C.
Cuba’s nuclear weapons marked its emergence as a superpower rivaling the United States and the Soviet Union.
The missiles demonstrated that Cuba had broken international laws barring the possession of nuclear weapons.

assessment_items.question_content:
The French decisively lost the First Indochina War in 1954 when Communist fighters for the People’s Army of Vietnam

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Vietnamese Communists did not destroy French ships.

choices.content: Conquered the northern city of Dien Bien Phu.
killed all of the French soldiers in the Battle of Ho Chi Minh City.
destroyed French ships as they attempted to enter the Gulf of Tonkin.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Cuban Missile Crisis** brought the United States and the USSR to the brink of a nuclear exchange; one result of the crisis was

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The atomic bomb the United States detonated at Nagasaki, Japan in 1945 is the last military use of a nuclear weapon to date.

choices.content: the installation of a Moscow-Washington hotline in the White House to forestall future crises.
an immediate worldwide ban on the production and sale of nuclear weapons
the last military use of a nuclear weapon to date when the US military detonated a bomb at Guantanamo Bay

assessment_items.question_content:
**Ho Chi Minh**, the leader of the **Viet Minh**, was

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the founder of Vietnam’s Communist Party, who opposed the French colonial presence.
the democratically-elected leader of South Vietnam
a Vietnamese Catholic priest who espoused non-violent resistance to colonial rule

assessment_items.question_content:
After successfully waging a guerilla war to depose Cuban dictator Fulgencio Batista and taking power himself in 1959, **Fidel Castro**’s relationship with the United States

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The United States and Cuba did not form a partnership to prevent  the spread of communism in Latin America.

choices.content: soured very quickly, as Castro increased his anti-US rhetoric and refused to hold elections
developed into a strong partnership to prevent the spread of communism in Latin America
warmed slowly; although Castro initially took a strong anti-American stance, he gradually built ties with the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the Kennedy administration react to discovering the presence of nuclear weapons in Cuba?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The US military did not fire warning shots from Turkey.

choices.content: They established a naval blockade of Cuba to prevent the Soviet Union from delivering more missiles.
They fired warning shots from the American missiles stationed in Turkey.
They dispatched secret agents to disarm the missiles.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why did the US government attempt the **Bay of Pigs** invasion?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The US government did not want to annex Cuba.

choices.content: The US government desired to overthrow Fidel Castro and replace him with someone friendlier to US interests.
The US government desired to annex Cuba in order to use the Bay of Pigs as a naval base.
The US government desired to unseat the Soviet puppet government installed by Nikita Khrushchev and hold democratic elections in Havana.

assessment_items.question_content:
Soviet Premier **Nikita Khrushchev** agreed to remove all of the USSR’s nuclear missiles from Cuban soil, leading to

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Khrushchev was never Secretary-General of the United Nations.

choices.content: his ouster as premier; other Soviet officials blamed him for bringing the world so close to nuclear war in the first place.
his promotion to head of the KGB, the Soviet equivalent of the CIA
his election as Secretary-General of the United Nations

assessment_items.question_content:
What was the outcome of the **Bay of Pigs invasion**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Bay of Pigs invasion was not a success.

choices.content: It was a failure; as a result of the invasion attempt, Cuba strengthened its ties with the USSR, leading to the Cuban Missile Crisis.
It was a success; the Cuban rebels ousted Castro and installed a US-backed government
It was a stalemate; although US forces managed to capture Guantanamo Bay, Castro remained in power in Cuba

assessment_items.question_content:
What motivated the **Apollo Missions** that would eventually send American astronauts to the moon?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Apollo missions were not the result of a budgetary surplus.

choices.content: A national desire to not be out-innovated by the Soviet Union
Growing concerns in the scientific community that resources on Earth would be insufficient to supply humans for much longer and that space colonization would soon be necessary
A budgetary surplus that the US government poured into scientific exploration

assessment_items.question_content:
Although the United States never formally declared war in Vietnam, in 1964, President Johnson _did_ declare a War On

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The War on Terror followed the terrorist attacks on September 11, 2001.

choices.content: Poverty
Drugs
Terror
